Machinery.--Robur had not availed himself of the vapor of water or other liquids, nor compressed air and other mechanical motion. He employed electricity, that agent which one day will be the soul of the industrial world. But he required no electro-motor to produce it. All he trusted to was piles and accumulators. What were the elements of these piles, and what were the acids he used, Robur only knew. And the construction of the accumulators was kept equally secret. Of what were their positive and negative plates? None can say. The engineer took good care--and not unreasonably--to keep his secret unpatented. One thing was unmistakable, and that was that the piles were of extraordinary strength; and the accumulators left those of Faure-Sellon-Volckmar very far behind in yielding currents whose ampères ran into figures up to then unknown. label: The Great Eyrie

As Gallia swings out toward the frozen depths and back again, the colonists endure cold and dwindling resources until the comet at last regrazes the Earth. Escaping by balloon at the moment of contact, the wanderers find themselves once more "in Algeria; and not far from Mostaganem" (OC-044-032, OC-045-002).

aliases: ['Un drame dans les airs', 'A Voyage in a Balloon', 'A Drama in the Clouds']

As they climb, the stranger pours out a long history of ballooning and its martyrs — Pilâtre des Rosiers, the drowning peril of Blanchard and Jeffries over the Channel, and the fiery death of "Madame Blanchard," who "was killed!" when her fireworks ignited the escaping gas (DA-001-118, DA-001-157).

Its magazines were bare when Hobson came, their stock "just been sent to the central factories in the south, so that the magazines of Fort Confidence were empty." (FC-008-006) Green pines frame its sharp cape against the water. (FC-008-037)

The Great Salt Lake is the real Utah lake that the transcontinental railroad skirts on Phileas Fogg's dash across America. The train enters "Utah, the region of the Great Salt Lake, the singular colony of the Mormons." (AWED-026-026)
- [[Off on a Comet]] — the novel of the comet voyage that carries the sea through space
